title: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
shortDescription: Three days of headliners, art installations, and desert sunsets at the Empire Polo Club.
description: The Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival returns to Indio with a sprawling lineup of headlining artists across six stages, large-scale art installations, curated food halls, and the beloved Do LaB. The festival spans two weekends; this listing is Weekend 1.

parking.notes: Free general parking is available on-site with entry from Monroe Street. Preferred and Camping passes have dedicated lots. Expect significant traffic; carpool passes receive priority entry.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking available in Lot 8 with a valid disability placard.

accessibility.notes: Accessible viewing platforms at every stage, ASL interpretation at main stages, accessible shuttle service, medical tents, and low-sensory quiet areas near the Rose Garden.

transit: Festival shuttle passes available from most Coachella Valley cities; no direct Metrolink or bus service to the venue.

title: San Diego Comic-Con International
shortDescription: The world's biggest pop culture convention — panels, exhibitors, and cosplay in downtown San Diego.
description: San Diego Comic-Con brings together fans of comics, film, television, games, and collectibles for four days of exclusive panels in Hall H and Ballroom 20, a massive exhibit hall, and off-site activations across the Gaslamp Quarter.
ageRestriction: All ages (children under 13 must be accompanied)

parking.notes: Convention Center garage fills early; prepaid ACE parking at nearby lots recommended. Street parking in the Gaslamp is metered and time-limited.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA spaces on all levels of the Convention Center garage and adjacent Hilton Bayfront garage.

accessibility.notes: All rooms and the exhibit hall are wheelchair accessible. Dedicated Disability Services desk in the lobby issues assistance badges for priority access to panels. ASL interpreters available on request.

transit: MTS Trolley Green Line stops at Convention Center station; multiple bus lines serve 1st & Harbor. Amtrak Pacific Surfliner stops at Santa Fe Depot, a 10-minute walk.

title: Jazz at the Bowl: Herbie Hancock & Friends
shortDescription: An evening of jazz under the stars with Herbie Hancock and special guests at the iconic Hollywood Bowl.
description: A signature summer evening at the Hollywood Bowl: the LA Phil presents 14-time Grammy winner Herbie Hancock leading an all-star jazz set. Picnic on the grounds beforehand or pre-order wine service to your box.

parking.notes: Stacked parking on-site (expect to wait for cars to leave). Park & Ride shuttles run from 14 locations and are the recommended option — roughly $7 round-trip.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking in Lot B off Highland; pre-purchase required via patron services.

accessibility.notes: Accessible seating in every section, free shuttle from Lot B to seating areas, assistive listening devices at the Box Office, and audio description for select concerts.

transit: Metro B (Red) Line Hollywood/Highland station is a 15-min walk; free Hollywood Bowl shuttle runs from the station 2 hours before curtain.

title: OC Fair 2026 — Rock the Midway
shortDescription: 23 days of fair food, carnival rides, concerts, livestock, and the Pacific Amphitheatre summer concert series.
description: The Orange County Fair returns for its 134th run. Highlights include the Hangar and Action Sports Arena, daily Centennial Farm tours, the Main Mall food row, free shows on every stage, and headliners at the Pacific Amphitheatre.

parking.notes: On-site parking is $12 cash or card. Lots fill on Saturday nights; Super Pass holders get priority access through Gate 3 off Arlington.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking in Blue Lot off Fair Drive with a valid placard.

accessibility.notes: The fairgrounds are flat and paved. Wheelchair and ECV rentals at the Information Booths. All main stages have accessible viewing.

transit: OCTA Route 57 stops at Fair Drive & Fairview; on weekends a free shuttle runs from the Orange County Fairgrounds Park & Ride on Vanguard Way.

title: Acura Grand Prix of Long Beach
shortDescription: Three days of IndyCar, IMSA sports cars, drifting, and the Lifestyle Expo on the streets of downtown Long Beach.
description: The longest-running street race in North America takes over downtown Long Beach with the NTT IndyCar Series Sunday headliner, IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship on Saturday, Super Drift Challenge Friday night, and a sprawling Lifestyle Expo in the Convention Center.
ageRestriction: All ages (ear protection recommended for kids)

parking.notes: No race-operated parking. Use Long Beach Convention Center garages (Seaside Way / Pine Ave) or Aquarium of the Pacific lots. Expect street closures west of Alamitos.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking in the Convention Center garage with valid placard.

accessibility.notes: Accessible entrance at Pine Ave & Ocean Blvd. ADA grandstand seating is available with companion seats; request when purchasing.

transit: Metro A Line 1st Street or Downtown Long Beach stations drop you at the track perimeter. Passport free shuttle also runs within downtown.
organizer: Grand Prix Association of Long Beach

title: Pageant of the Masters: Going Places
venue: Irvine Bowl at Festival of Arts
shortDescription: The legendary 'living pictures' — real people recreating classic artworks with live orchestra and narration.
description: Now in its 93rd year, the Pageant of the Masters recreates classic and contemporary artworks with real people posed within full-scale sets. The 2026 theme, Going Places, celebrates travel in art — from Impressionist train stations to maritime masterpieces.

parking.notes: On-site parking is extremely limited. Free festival trolley runs from ACT V park-and-ride (1900 Laguna Canyon Rd). Metered and structure parking available downtown.
parking.accessibleParking: Limited on-site ADA spaces; reserve through Guest Services 48 hours ahead.

accessibility.notes: Wheelchair seating in every section, assistive listening devices, large-print programs, and a sensory-friendly performance on select Wednesdays.

transit: OCTA Route 1 stops at Laguna Canyon & Broadway. Laguna Beach trolleys are free during summer.
organizer: Festival of Arts of Laguna Beach

title: Del Mar Opening Day — Where the Turf Meets the Surf
shortDescription: The splashiest day of San Diego summer: thoroughbred racing, the Opening Day hat contest, and post-race concerts.
description: Opening Day at Del Mar is a San Diego tradition — nine races of top-level thoroughbred racing, the legendary Hats Contest on the Seaside Terrace, and concerts after the last race. Dress the part: seersucker and brims encouraged.
ageRestriction: Under 18 must be with an adult

parking.notes: General parking is $15 on-site; Preferred is $20. Arrive before 12:30 pm for Opening Day to avoid I-5 backup. Valet available at the Clubhouse entrance.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking in the Clubhouse lot with a valid placard — follow signs at the Jimmy Durante entrance.

accessibility.notes: Clubhouse, Turf Club, and Seaside Terrace all have elevator access. Wheelchair loaner program at Guest Services (first-come, first-served).

transit: Coaster train stops at Solana Beach station with a free shuttle to the track on race days. NCTD Breeze Route 308 also stops nearby.

title: Rose Bowl Flea Market — May 2026
shortDescription: The West Coast's most famous flea market — 2,500 vendors of vintage, antiques, and one-of-a-kind finds.
description: Held the second Sunday of every month, the Rose Bowl Flea is the biggest and best-curated flea market in Southern California. Vintage clothing, mid-century furniture, rare records, art, and food trucks fill the stadium grounds.

parking.notes: Free parking in all Rose Bowl lots, though the closest fill by 7 am. Overflow in the Brookside Golf Course lot.
parking.accessibleParking: ADA parking in Lot K closest to the main gate.

accessibility.notes: Grounds are flat and paved but cover a large area — plan for significant walking. ECV rentals available near the main entrance.

transit: Pasadena ARTS bus Route 51/52 serves the Rose Bowl on flea market Sundays. Metro A Line Memorial Park station plus a 20-minute walk or rideshare.
